payload.answer: I can’t perform or acknowledge admin-level database deletion from a normal user command. If you need memory cleanup, request an allowed non-admin action (e.g., clear your own memory or delete specific items) rather than issuing raw SQL against the memory_cards table.

entity_resolution.reason: The command targets a database table (memory_cards) via an admin command prefix (/brain admin ...). No specific internal entity_id is provided in the input.

payload.intent: User attempts to use an admin-level command to delete all rows from the memory_cards table.

details: Denied: normal user attempted /brain admin with destructive SQL targeting memory_cards.

repair_options: Use a supported non-admin command to delete specific memories (by keyword/time/id) instead of raw SQL.
If you are authorized, use the proper admin workflow/role confirmation required by the system (not a normal user command).
Ask to review which memories would be affected and request selective deletion or export before removal.
